approval_notes: Contract status changed to approved only after the working tree was diagnosed clean, diagnostic snapshots were written outside the repository, and standalone line-ending normalization was committed.
objective: Establish the F000 harness foundation so a fresh agent can start from repository artifacts, run canonical setup checks, work one increment, and leave independently evaluable handoff state.
scope: Maintain the root .gitattributes policy that normalizes detected text files to LF while preserving CRLF for native Windows command scripts.
Keep repository line-ending normalization as an explicit F000 foundation requirement.
Implement and maintain scripts/init.sh and scripts/init.ps1 when F000 continues.
Require initialization scripts to resolve the repository root from their own script path instead of the caller's current directory.
Require PowerShell initialization to report unverified when neither pwsh nor Windows PowerShell is available.
Add baseline smoke-test commands once the application scaffold exists.
Ensure negative JSON validation tests use temporary copies or temporary fixtures and never corrupt active repository state.
Have the builder update only the builder progress entry and leave agent-state/evaluation.json pending.
Require a fresh evaluator to perform and record the independent evaluation.
out_of_scope: Application scaffolding, provider adapters, catalogue UI, API, workers, scheduler, database schema, and deployment work.
Changing feature ledger descriptions or acceptance steps outside explicit owner-approved F000 contract updates.
Marking F000 as passing before all acceptance criteria and independent evaluation succeed.
Recording evaluator results from the builder context.
acceptance_criteria: A root .gitattributes file exists and encodes the approved line-ending policy.
git add --renormalize . has been run after adding .gitattributes, and staged normalization is inspected with status, stat, check, and representative diffs.
Normalization does not alter textual content beyond line endings.
scripts/init.sh and scripts/init.ps1 resolve the repository root from their own path.
PowerShell initialization reports unverified when neither pwsh nor Windows PowerShell is available.
The canonical initialization path documents bootstrap-incomplete status until runnable setup and smoke scripts exist.
Negative JSON tests operate on a temporary copy or temporary fixture and leave active repository state unchanged.
The builder appends only the builder progress entry and leaves agent-state/evaluation.json pending.
A fresh evaluator records evaluation in agent-state/evaluation.json before F000 can pass.

end_to_end_steps: Start from a clean checkout and read AGENTS.md, docs/AGENT_HARNESS.md, agent-state/progress.md, agent-state/feature_list.json, and recent git history.
Run the canonical initialization script when present; until scripts exist, record bootstrap-incomplete status.
Run the baseline smoke workflow when the application scaffold exists; until then, record scaffold-not-present status.
Create or review the F000 current contract and verify it names scope, out-of-scope work, acceptance criteria, risks, and evaluator requirements.
Append a builder handoff to agent-state/progress.md without writing evaluator disposition beyond pending.
negative_tests: Attempt JSON validation against a temporary malformed copy or fixture and verify the active repository JSON files are unchanged.
Run initialization from a directory outside the repository and verify scripts still resolve the repository root from their own path.
Simulate missing PowerShell hosts where practical and verify the result is reported as unverified rather than passed.

risks.data_migration: None; F000 does not introduce application or database state.
risks.security_privacy: No secrets or user data are introduced. Future scripts must avoid logging secrets and must not mutate active JSON during negative tests.
risks.portability: Initialization must work from script-relative paths across supported shells. PowerShell availability must be reported accurately, including unverified when unavailable.
risks.zero_cost_safety: No hosting or paid dependency is introduced. F000 must not claim any service is free.
risks.regression: Line-ending normalization can create noisy diffs if misconfigured; .gitattributes and renormalization inspection are required to keep future changes reviewable.

notes: Owner explicitly approved amending F000 to include .gitattributes, line-ending normalization, builder/evaluator boundaries, script root resolution, PowerShell unverified behavior, and safe negative JSON tests.
